---
title: "Close Reading Study - Main Study Analysis"
author: "Jiayin Zhi"
date: "2026"
output: 
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
    toc_float: true
    df_print: paged
---

# Setup
```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(ordinal)
library(broom.mixed)
library(knitr)
library(glue)

setwd(dirname(rstudioapi::getSourceEditorContext()$path))
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = normalizePath("."))
set.seed(234)
```

# 1. Data Loading and Preparation
## 1.1 Load Data
```{r load-data, message=FALSE}
# Load datasets; files should be in the same directory as this script
df_control  <- read_excel("data/Control_APPROVED.xlsx")
df_single   <- read_excel("data/AI_Single_APPROVED.xlsx")
df_multiple <- read_excel("data/AI_Multiple_APPROVED.xlsx")

# Combine all conditions
df_all <- bind_rows(df_control, df_single, df_multiple) %>%
  rename(participant_id = anonymized_id) %>%
  mutate(condition = factor(Condition, levels = c("Control", "AI_Single", "AI_Multiple"))) %>%
  select(-Condition)

cat("Total participants:", nrow(df_all), "\n")
cat("Participants per condition:\n")
print(table(df_all$condition))

df_clean <- df_all
```

## 1.2 Create Expertise Classification
```{r expertise-classification}
course_exp_levels <- c("None", "Very little", "A little", "A moderate amount", "A lot")

df_clean <- df_clean %>%
  mutate(
    humanities_course_experience = factor(
      humanities_course_experience,
      levels = course_exp_levels,
      ordered = TRUE
    ),
    # Binary expertise: "A moderate amount" or higher = expert
    humanities_expert = factor(
      if_else(as.numeric(humanities_course_experience) >= 4, "expert", "novice"),
      levels = c("novice", "expert")
    )
  )

cat("Expertise distribution:\n")
print(table(df_clean$humanities_expert, useNA = "always"))
cat("\nExpertise by condition:\n")
print(table(df_clean$condition, df_clean$humanities_expert))
```

# 2. Performance Measures Analysis
## 2.1 Load Graded Performance Data
```{r load-graded-data, message=FALSE}
graded_data <- read_excel("grades/Grades.xlsx")
col_names   <- colnames(graded_data)

graded_clean <- graded_data %>%
  rename(
    participant_id = anonymized_id,
    # Love Poem scores
    love_poem_first_feature          = !!sym(col_names[4]),
    love_poem_second_feature         = !!sym(col_names[9]),
    love_poem_third_feature          = !!sym(col_names[14]),
    love_poem_first_interpretation   = !!sym(col_names[5]),
    love_poem_second_interpretation  = !!sym(col_names[10]),
    love_poem_third_interpretation   = !!sym(col_names[15]),
    love_poem_first_writing          = !!sym(col_names[6]),
    love_poem_second_writing         = !!sym(col_names[11]),
    love_poem_third_writing          = !!sym(col_names[16]),
    # Dusting scores
    dusting_first_feature            = !!sym(col_names[19]),
    dusting_second_feature           = !!sym(col_names[24]),
    dusting_third_feature            = !!sym(col_names[29]),
    dusting_first_interpretation     = !!sym(col_names[20]),
    dusting_second_interpretation    = !!sym(col_names[25]),
    dusting_third_interpretation     = !!sym(col_names[30]),
    dusting_first_writing            = !!sym(col_names[21]),
    dusting_second_writing           = !!sym(col_names[26]),
    dusting_third_writing            = !!sym(col_names[31]),
    # English B scores
    english_b_first_feature          = !!sym(col_names[34]),
    english_b_second_feature         = !!sym(col_names[39]),
    english_b_third_feature          = !!sym(col_names[44]),
    english_b_first_interpretation   = !!sym(col_names[35]),
    english_b_second_interpretation  = !!sym(col_names[40]),
    english_b_third_interpretation   = !!sym(col_names[45]),
    english_b_first_writing          = !!sym(col_names[36]),
    english_b_second_writing         = !!sym(col_names[41]),
    english_b_third_writing          = !!sym(col_names[46])
  )
```

## 2.2 Create Poem-Level Performance Scores
```{r create-performance-data}
poems <- c("love_poem", "dusting", "english_b")

df_performance_poem_level <- map_dfr(poems, function(p) {
  feature_cols <- paste0(p, c("_first_feature", "_second_feature", "_third_feature"))
  interp_cols  <- paste0(p, c("_first_interpretation", "_second_interpretation", "_third_interpretation"))
  writing_cols <- paste0(p, c("_first_writing", "_second_writing", "_third_writing"))

  graded_clean %>%
    select(participant_id, all_of(feature_cols), all_of(interp_cols), all_of(writing_cols)) %>%
    rowwise() %>%
    mutate(
      feature_score        = mean(c_across(all_of(feature_cols)), na.rm = TRUE),
      interpretation_score = mean(c_across(all_of(interp_cols)),  na.rm = TRUE),
      writing_score        = mean(c_across(all_of(writing_cols)), na.rm = TRUE),
      poem_id = p
    ) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    select(participant_id, poem_id, feature_score, interpretation_score, writing_score)
})

df_performance <- df_performance_poem_level %>%
  left_join(
    df_clean %>% select(participant_id, condition, humanities_expert, poem_order),
    by = "participant_id"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(condition)) %>%
  mutate(
    condition            = factor(condition, levels = c("Control", "AI_Single", "AI_Multiple")),
    poem_id              = factor(poem_id, levels = c("love_poem", "dusting", "english_b")),
    participant_expertise = factor(humanities_expert, levels = c("novice", "expert")),
    poem_position = factor(
      sapply(seq_along(poem_id), function(i) {
        order_vec <- strsplit(poem_order[i], ";")[[1]]
        match(as.character(poem_id[i]), order_vec)
      }),
      levels = c("1", "2", "3")
    )
  )

cat("Performance data dimensions:", dim(df_performance), "\n")
```

## 2.3 Mixed-Effects Models for Performance
```{r performance-models}
model_feature <- lmer(
  feature_score ~ condition + poem_position + poem_id + participant_expertise +
    (1 | participant_id),
  data = df_performance
)

model_interpretation <- lmer(
  interpretation_score ~ condition + poem_position + poem_id + participant_expertise +
    (1 | participant_id),
  data = df_performance
)

model_writing <- lmer(
  writing_score ~ condition + poem_position + poem_id + participant_expertise +
    (1 | participant_id),
  data = df_performance
)

performance_results <- bind_rows(
  tidy(model_feature,        effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE) %>% mutate(outcome = "Feature Identification"),
  tidy(model_interpretation, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE) %>% mutate(outcome = "Interpretation Quality"),
  tidy(model_writing,        effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE) %>% mutate(outcome = "Writing Quality")
) %>%
  select(outcome, term, estimate, std.error, statistic, p.value, conf.low, conf.high) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)")

kable(performance_results, digits = 4,
      caption = "Mixed-Effects Models: Performance Measures")

kable(performance_results %>% filter(grepl("condition", term)), digits = 4,
      caption = "Summary: Condition Effects on Performance")
```

# 3. Subjective Experience Analysis
## 3.1 Create Long-Format Data for Ordinal Outcomes
```{r create-ordinal-data}
appreciation_levels <- c(
  "I had a strongly negative reaction to it",
  "I had a moderately negative reaction to it",
  "I had a slightly negative reaction to it",
  "Neutral",
  "I had a slightly positive reaction to it",
  "I had a moderately positive reaction to it",
  "I had a strongly positive reaction to it"
)

enjoyment_levels <- c(
  "Very unenjoyable",
  "Moderately unenjoyable",
  "Slightly unenjoyable",
  "Neither enjoyable nor unenjoyable",
  "Slightly enjoyable",
  "Moderately enjoyable",
  "Very enjoyable"
)

self_efficacy_levels <- c(
  "I feel strongly unable",
  "I feel moderately unable",
  "I feel slightly unable",
  "I feel neither able nor unable",
  "I feel slightly able",
  "I feel moderately able",
  "I feel strongly able"
)

create_long_data <- function(df, measure_suffix, levels) {
  map_dfr(poems, function(p) {
    col_name <- glue("{p}_close_reading_{measure_suffix}")
    df %>%
      select(participant_id, condition, poem_order, humanities_expert, !!sym(col_name)) %>%
      rename(rating = !!sym(col_name)) %>%
      mutate(
        poem_id        = p,
        rating_factor  = factor(rating, levels = levels, ordered = TRUE),
        rating_numeric = as.numeric(rating_factor)
      )
  }) %>%
    filter(!is.na(rating_numeric))
}

prepare_ordinal_data <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    mutate(
      condition             = factor(condition, levels = c("Control", "AI_Single", "AI_Multiple")),
      participant_expertise = factor(humanities_expert, levels = c("novice", "expert")),
      poem_id               = factor(poem_id, levels = c("love_poem", "dusting", "english_b")),
      poem_position = factor(
        sapply(seq_along(poem_id), function(i) {
          order_vec <- strsplit(poem_order[i], ";")[[1]]
          match(as.character(poem_id[i]), order_vec)
        }),
        levels = c("1", "2", "3")
      )
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(rating_numeric), !is.na(participant_expertise))
}

df_appreciation  <- create_long_data(df_clean, "appreciation",  appreciation_levels)  %>% prepare_ordinal_data()
df_enjoyment     <- create_long_data(df_clean, "enjoyment",     enjoyment_levels)     %>% prepare_ordinal_data()
df_self_efficacy <- create_long_data(df_clean, "self_efficacy", self_efficacy_levels) %>% prepare_ordinal_data()
```

## 3.2 Ordinal Mixed-Effects Models
```{r ordinal-models}
model_appreciation <- clmm(
  rating_factor ~ condition + poem_position + poem_id + participant_expertise +
    (1 | participant_id),
  data = df_appreciation
)

model_enjoyment <- clmm(
  rating_factor ~ condition + poem_position + poem_id + participant_expertise +
    (1 | participant_id),
  data = df_enjoyment
)

model_self_efficacy <- clmm(
  rating_factor ~ condition + poem_position + poem_id + participant_expertise +
    (1 | participant_id),
  data = df_self_efficacy
)

extract_ordinal_results <- function(model, model_name) {
  tidy(model, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE) %>%
    mutate(outcome = model_name) %>%
    filter(!grepl("\\|", term))
}

ordinal_results <- bind_rows(
  extract_ordinal_results(model_appreciation,  "Appreciation"),
  extract_ordinal_results(model_enjoyment,     "Enjoyment"),
  extract_ordinal_results(model_self_efficacy, "Self-Efficacy")
) %>%
  select(outcome, term, estimate, std.error, statistic, p.value, conf.low, conf.high)

kable(ordinal_results, digits = 3,
      caption = "Ordinal Mixed Models: Subjective Experience (Log-Odds)")

kable(ordinal_results %>% filter(grepl("condition", term)), digits = 3,
      caption = "Summary: Condition Effects on Subjective Experience")
```

# 4. Session Information
```{r session-info}
sessionInfo()
```
